mnemonics.title: Remember Format Flags
mnemonics.content: FLASh RISE: Fixed, Left, Adjustfield, Showbase, Hex, Right, Internal, Showpoint, Scientific, Eq (equal/uppercase), noskipws, Showpos, Boolalpha

acronyms.usage: C++ standard library for input/output operations
Sets minimum field width for output

visualTips.visualization: Think of each output as a box with a specified width. Numbers fill from right by default, empty space gets filled with spaces (or setfill character).
Imagine precision as the 'zoom level' on a number. Higher precision = more digits visible. With fixed, it's like a ruler measuring exact decimal places.
Think of different number bases as different languages: decimal is English (0-9), octal is Spanish (0-7), hex is French (0-9, A-F). The manipulators switch between these languages.
Output buffer is like a delivery truck. cout is a scheduled delivery (buffered), cerr is an emergency delivery (immediate), clog is a scheduled but tracked delivery.

analogies.analogy: Like a fixed-width ticket or parking space - if your number doesn't fill it completely, the system adds padding (spaces or custom character) to fill the space.
Like camera resolution. setprecision(3) is like a 3-megapixel camera - only captures 3 significant 'details' of the number.
\n is like pressing Enter in a text editor (just adds line break). endl is like pressing Enter AND sending the document to the printer immediately.
cout is like sending a letter through regular mail (it sits in a collection bin). cerr is like calling 911 - goes through immediately without waiting.
Think of it as a 'translate' toggle - either shows the number (1/0) or the word (true/false). It's like Google Translate between numeric and verbal boolean representation.
fixed is writing money in dollars and cents format ($12.34). scientific is like scientific notation on calculators (1.234e+01).
